scope.manual_review: Every strict peer API row and every resulting domain overlap was checked against its public YouTube page. Tracking links were resolved where present.

placement_classes.S3: Confirmed paid sponsorship or partnership: the brand is named and the public page contains an unambiguous sponsor or partner disclosure, or an attributable YouTube paid-promotion declaration.
placement_classes.S2: Confirmed brand promotion or showcase, but compensation is not publicly established.
placement_classes.S1: Affiliate link, product mention, or monetized product placement only.

product_line_continuity.A: Exact continuity: the same product family, model line, or direct successor, within the same business unit and buyer use case.
product_line_continuity.B: Adjacent continuity: different lines within the same business unit and buyer use case, with a credible campaign progression.

rubric.strict_pass_gate: Target S3 plus at least one peer S3 plus product-line continuity A or B.
exclusions: A coupon or affiliate link alone cannot qualify as S3.
A conglomerate root-domain match cannot pass when the placements belong to different business units.
The result set is not padded when fewer than three matches pass.

peer_inventory.public_evidence: The description explicitly says Dell sponsored the video, names XPS 13, and uses #DellCollab.
peer_inventory.verdict: Confirmed Dell sponsorship in the XPS consumer-laptop family.

peer_inventory.video_title: I Bought AI Generated Apple Products
peer_inventory.public_evidence: The description labels Wispr Flow as the sponsor, uses #WisprFlowPartner, and gives a creator promo code.
peer_inventory.verdict: Confirmed sponsorship; no stale target-domain match.

peer_inventory.video_title: Where Ferrari TESTS ALL THEIR CARS!! 🏎️
peer_inventory.public_evidence: The page declares paid promotion and thanks Bitdefender for arranging the Italy experience; no sponsor destination is provided.
peer_inventory.product_line: Bitdefender brand activation; specific product not stated
peer_inventory.verdict: Confirmed paid brand activation, but excluded from the domain join because no attributable sponsor domain was present.

overlaps.continuity_reason: Both placements explicitly promote the XPS laptop family to consumer technology buyers. The model differs, but the family, business unit, and buyer use case are the same.

target_evidence.public_evidence: The description thanks Dell for sponsoring the video, links the XPS laptop offer, and uses #DellCollab.

peer_evidence.public_evidence: The description thanks Dell for sponsoring the video, names XPS 13, links the XPS family page, and uses #DellCollab.

results.reason: The target evidence was an affiliate/product placement (S1), not an S3 sponsorship.
The large-peer evidence was a generic product affiliate link (S1), not an S3 Dell sponsorship.
Neither side had an unambiguous paid-sponsorship disclosure.
The peer placement was not confirmed S3 and the product lines crossed home appliances and enterprise laptops (continuity C).
The target was S3, but the large-peer placement was a creator-code promotion without confirmed paid sponsorship (S2).

brand_research_assessment.what_it_added: A concise description of Dell's consumer and enterprise footprint, professional voice, broad audience, and technology positioning.
brand_research_assessment.what_it_did_not_add: It did not identify the XPS campaign, current product priority, media buyer, agency, creator brief, geography, or budget.
brand_research_assessment.continuity_source: The A-grade XPS continuity comes from the two manually verified placements and their destination pages, not from Brand Research.
